Ground READ-ONLY against internal/bind/multipath.go (ps.txBytes.Add sites Send/fecFlushDeadline, emitProbes probe.go, echo reflection; NewMultipath has no logger param), internal/device/device.go (reloadWarnings), internal/bind/pathsock.go (listenPath SO_BINDTODEVICE fallback), internal/metrics.

G10 is DEFECT-SEEDED. Produce a candidate task-DAG of coherent fix tasks. NOTE: D48 and D53 both touch internal/bind/multipath.go (D48 txBytes counting in Send/emitProbes; D53 threads a logger through NewMultipath into listenPath) — consider serialization or a shared bind-observability task to avoid worktree merge conflicts in the 2820-line multipath.go. D52 is internal/device (reloadWarnings) — separate. Fixes: D48 count probe/echo into ps.txBytes (or re-document the metric) + flip T104's standby-transmits-when-idle subtest to green; D52 extend reloadWarnings with DeepEqual over Scheduler/FEC/DNS/Bind (or a zeroed-copy comparison future-proof) + unit tests; D53 thread internal/log through NewMultipath into listenPath/planPathBinds/resolveForcedDeviceBind + WARN on every forced-device→source fallback (covers the pre-existing CAP fallback). 18	// syscall nor blocks the lock-free receive fast path. A concurrent Close closes
19	// the sockets out from under the snapshot; the resulting write error is benign
20	// (teardown) and dropped. It is a no-op when the bind has no probers or is closed.

23		if len(m.paths) == 0 || m.probers == nil {
24			m.mu.Unlock()
25			return
26		}

28			ps *peerPathState
29			pr *telemetry.Prober
30		}

32		// each edge over that edge-peer's per-(peer,path) prober, so every peer's liveness/RTT is
33		// measured for its OWN scheduler. On the single-peer edge/hub m.peers holds only the
34		// primary, so this is byte-identical to the pre-split single-peer sweep.
35		targets := make([]target, 0, len(m.paths))
36		for _, p := range m.peers {
37			for _, ps := range p.paths {
38				if ps.prober == nil {
39					continue
40				}
41				targets = append(targets, target{ps: ps, pr: ps.prober})

43		}

45

48				if raw, err := t.pr.SendProbe(); err == nil {
49					// UDP writes are goroutine-safe; this races no in-flight Send.
50					_, _ = t.ps.conn.WriteToUDPAddrPort(raw, remote)

56		// each probe cadence so a liveness DOWN switches egress even when no application
57		// Send is driving Pick. This is the timer-driven companion to the receive-tick
58		// nudge (see tickLivenessFromReceive) — under CPU starvation this loop's ticker

65	// interval until the returned stop function is invoked. The caller (device.Up)
66	// starts it AFTER the engine has opened the bind and stops it BEFORE Close, so the
67	// loop only runs while the sockets exist (emitProbes is a safe no-op if it races a
68	// closed bind). The returned stopper is idempotent.

75	// probers or interval <= 0.

81		// now advance liveness when the timer goroutine below is starved under load.

88				select {
89				case <-done:
90					return
91				case <-ticker.C:
92					m.emitProbes()
93				}
94			}
95		}()

97		return func() { once.Do(func() { close(done) }) }

87	//   - RAISE (s >= RaiseThreshold): m may only INCREASE, toward the safety-factored
88	//     redundancy map. A decrease here would defeat the raise band, so it is
89	//     forbidden; this is why m does not oscillate while loss stays elevated.
90	//   - LOWER (s <= LowerThreshold): m COLLAPSES toward 0 — but only after the loss
91	//     has stayed quiet for the full Dwell. Being below the lower band is exactly
92	//     the "near-zero loss" condition, so the shed target is 0 (zero-at-zero), NOT
93	//     the raw map: the map's ceil() would pin any infinitesimal EWMA tail at M=1
94	//     and standing redundancy would never fully clear on a clean path.
95	//   - DEADBAND (in between): HOLD. A loss signal oscillating inside [Lower,Raise]
96	//     cannot move m, so it cannot flap.
97	//
98	// The raise-fast / lower-after-dwell asymmetry is the anti-thrash core.
99	func (c *Controller) bandTarget(s float64, now time.Time) int {
100		switch {
101		case s >= c.cfg.RaiseThreshold:
102			return maxInt(c.m, c.redundancyMap(s))
103		case s <= c.cfg.LowerThreshold:
104			if c.haveLowSince && now.Sub(c.lowSince) >= c.cfg.Dwell {
105				return 0
106			}
107			return c.m
108		default:
109			return c.m
110		}

113	// redundancyMap converts a smoothed loss fraction into the parity count M. In the
114	// RESIDUAL-SLA mode (TargetResidual set) it delegates to residualTargetParity,
115	// which inverts the binomial residual model to the smallest M meeting the target.
116	// Otherwise it uses the legacy SafetyFactor map: the smallest M whose group
117	// tolerates safety*loss, i.e. M/(K+M) >= safety*loss:
118	//
119	//	M = ceil( K * e / (1 - e) ),  e = safety*loss
120	//
121	// e saturates to MaxParity as e -> 1, and loss <= 0 maps to exactly 0 (zero
122	// overhead when clean). The result is clamped to [0, MaxParity]. Both modes are
123	// monotone non-decreasing and zero-at-zero, so the surrounding hysteresis and
124	// slew machinery is identical regardless of which mode is active.
125	func (c *Controller) redundancyMap(loss float64) int {
126		if c.cfg.TargetResidual > 0 {
127			return c.residualTargetParity(loss)
128		}
129		e := loss * c.cfg.SafetyFactor
130		if e <= 0 {
131			return 0
132		}
133		if e >= 1 {
134			return c.cfg.MaxParity
135		}
136		m := int(math.Ceil(float64(c.cfg.DataShards) * e / (1 - e)))
137		if m < 0 {
138			m = 0
139		}
140		if m > c.cfg.MaxParity {

142		}
143		return m
144	}

148	// previous change. The first change is permitted immediately.

152		}

154			return
155		}

48	// Clock), into the controller and returns the resulting target parity count M.
49	// A loss outside [0,1] (including NaN) is a telemetry boundary artifact and is
50	// clamped rather than rejected, so a transient bad estimate cannot destabilize
51	// the loop.
52	func (c *Controller) Observe(loss float64) int {
53		now := c.clock.Now()
54		s := c.updateEWMA(clampLoss(loss))
55	
56		// Track how long smoothed loss has stayed in the lower (shed-eligible) region;
57		// the dwell for a decrease is measured from lowSince.
58		if s <= c.cfg.LowerThreshold {
59			if !c.haveLowSince {
60				c.lowSince = now
61				c.haveLowSince = true
62			}
63		} else {
64			c.haveLowSince = false
65		}
66	
67		target := c.bandTarget(s, now)
68		c.slewToward(target, now)
69		return c.m
70	}
71	
72	// updateEWMA folds one clamped sample into the smoothed loss and returns it. The
73	// first sample seeds the average directly.
74	func (c *Controller) updateEWMA(loss float64) float64 {
75		if !c.haveEWMA {
76			c.smoothed = loss
77			c.haveEWMA = true
78		} else {
79			c.smoothed = c.cfg.Alpha*loss + (1-c.cfg.Alpha)*c.smoothed
80		}
81		return c.smoothed
82	}
83	
84	// bandTarget applies the hysteresis bands to pick the parity level m should move
85	// toward, giving the loop three regions:
86	//
87	//   - RAISE (s >= RaiseThreshold): m may only INCREASE, toward the safety-factored
88	//     redundancy map. A decrease here would defeat the raise band, so it is
89	//     forbidden; this is why m does not oscillate while loss stays elevated.
90	//   - LOWER (s <= LowerThreshold): m COLLAPSES toward 0 — but only after the loss
91	//     has stayed quiet for the full Dwell. Being below the lower band is exactly
92	//     the "near-zero loss" condition, so the shed target is 0 (zero-at-zero), NOT
93	//     the raw map: the map's ceil() would pin any infinitesimal EWMA tail at M=1
94	//     and standing redundancy would never fully clear on a clean path.
95	//   - DEADBAND (in between): HOLD. A loss signal oscillating inside [Lower,Raise]
96	//     cannot move m, so it cannot flap.
97	//
98	// The raise-fast / lower-after-dwell asymmetry is the anti-thrash core.
99	func (c *Controller) bandTarget(s float64, now time.Time) int {
100		switch {
101		case s >= c.cfg.RaiseThreshold:
102			return maxInt(c.m, c.redundancyMap(s))
103		case s <= c.cfg.LowerThreshold:
104			if c.haveLowSince && now.Sub(c.lowSince) >= c.cfg.Dwell {
105				return 0
106			}
107			return c.m
108		default:
109			return c.m
110		}
111	}
112	
113	// redundancyMap converts a smoothed loss fraction into the parity count M. In the
114	// RESIDUAL-SLA mode (TargetResidual set) it delegates to residualTargetParity,
115	// which inverts the binomial residual model to the smallest M meeting the target.
116	// Otherwise it uses the legacy SafetyFactor map: the smallest M whose group
117	// tolerates safety*loss, i.e. M/(K+M) >= safety*loss:
118	//
119	//	M = ceil( K * e / (1 - e) ),  e = safety*loss
120	//
121	// e saturates to MaxParity as e -> 1, and loss <= 0 maps to exactly 0 (zero
122	// overhead when clean). The result is clamped to [0, MaxParity]. Both modes are
123	// monotone non-decreasing and zero-at-zero, so the surrounding hysteresis and
124	// slew machinery is identical regardless of which mode is active.
125	func (c *Controller) redundancyMap(loss float64) int {
126		if c.cfg.TargetResidual > 0 {
127			return c.residualTargetParity(loss)
128		}
129		e := loss * c.cfg.SafetyFactor
130		if e <= 0 {
131			return 0
132		}
133		if e >= 1 {
134			return c.cfg.MaxParity
135		}
136		m := int(math.Ceil(float64(c.cfg.DataShards) * e / (1 - e)))
137		if m < 0 {
138			m = 0
139		}
140		if m > c.cfg.MaxParity {
141			m = c.cfg.MaxParity
142		}
143		return m
144	}
145	
146	// slewToward moves m toward target subject to the rate limit: m changes by at
147	// most MaxStep, and no change may occur until RateInterval has elapsed since the
148	// previous change. The first change is permitted immediately.
149	func (c *Controller) slewToward(target int, now time.Time) {
150		if target == c.m {
151			return
152		}
153		if c.haveChanged && now.Sub(c.lastChange) < c.cfg.RateInterval {
154			return
155		}
156		step := target - c.m
157		if step > c.cfg.MaxStep {
158			step = c.cfg.MaxStep
159		} else if step < -c.cfg.MaxStep {
160			step = -c.cfg.MaxStep
161		}
162		c.m += step
163		c.lastChange = now
164		c.haveChanged = true
165	}
166	
167	// Parity returns the current target parity count M without advancing state.
168	func (c *Controller) Parity() int { return c.m }
169	
170	// SmoothedLoss returns the current EWMA loss estimate. This is a read-only
171	// signal a future integration MAY also feed to the weighted scheduler (T21);
172	// this package wires nothing.
173	func (c *Controller) SmoothedLoss() float64 { return c.smoothed }
174	
175	// Overhead returns the current parity fraction M/(K+M): the fraction of on-wire
176	// shards that are parity at the present target. 0 when M is 0.
177	func (c *Controller) Overhead() float64 {
178		if c.m == 0 {
179			return 0
180		}
181		return float64(c.m) / float64(c.cfg.DataShards+c.m)
182	}
183	
184	func clampLoss(loss float64) float64 {
185		if math.IsNaN(loss) || loss < 0 {
186			return 0
187		}
188		if loss > 1 {
189			return 1
190		}
191		return loss
192	}
193	
194	func maxInt(a, b int) int {
195		if a > b {
196			return a
197		}
198		return b
199	}
